def: To have the most recent information or knowledge.
Energy from sources that do not run out, like sun and wind.
Gases or substances released into the air, especially pollution.
An agreement reached when each side gives up something.
To formally approve a new law in parliament.
A person who is in charge of others at work.
A separate section of a company or organisation.
A discussion about how much someone will be paid.
Careful study done to discover new facts.
A test done to discover or prove something.
Causing damage or harm.
A violent tropical storm in the western Pacific.
The layer of clouds covering the sky.
Emotionally stirred up and restless.
A large sea animal with a hard shell and strong claws, eaten as food.
A country of seven states on the Arabian Peninsula.
Feeling gentle emotion, close to tears.
Not willing to trust; suspicious.
To feel a strong wish for something or someone.
A state of deep exhaustion caused by long-term stress.
Overcome by a strong feeling or by too much at once.
A unit in a cell that carries inherited traits from parents.
A cupboard built into the wall of a room.
The service that takes away household rubbish.
Having many people living close together in an area.
The state of having nowhere to live.
The planned growth and improvement of a city.
Active involvement and effort in a cause or task.
A building where soldiers live.
To move around an area to guard or watch it.
To find and enlist new members or workers.
Planned carefully to achieve a long-term aim.
The main centre from which an organisation is run.
A slab of snow that breaks loose and slides down a slope.
A general direction or inclination toward something.
One particular part or feature of something.
A statement about what will happen in the future.
A judgement reached by reasoning from facts.
The way something is noticed or understood through the senses.
The pleasant feeling when worry or pain goes away.
A rise in amount, size, or number.
The typical or middle value found by adding and dividing.
To have an effect on how something develops or behaves.
Existing in large numbers; many.
For the time being; not yet final.
Causing strong disagreement among people.
By contrast; however.
On the condition that.
Given special stress or importance.
Up to the present time.
Once more; another time.
On the first occasion.
From now on; in the time to come.
A group joined together for a common purpose.
As before; also in addition.
A question or request for information.
Claimed to be true, but not proven.
A formal statement that someone has committed a crime.
A formal word meaning 'or rather' or 'respectively'.
As a direct result; immediately after that.
In the opposite direction to; contrary to.
Matching or suitable to something; accordingly.
A person who looks into a crime or matter to find facts.
The provision of money for a project or purpose.
An organisation that protects workers' rights.
Concerning basic principles; as a general rule.
A first step or plan to deal with a problem; also drive.
Not long ago.
As great as possible; at the upper limit.
Happening or existing more than once.
A person who takes over a role from another.
The people who work for an organisation.
A meeting where news is given to journalists.
The people who prepare a publication; also the editing work.
A formal gathering to discuss or decide matters.
An organisation set up to support a cause with its funds.
The act of putting a plan or idea into practice.
A set way of doing something; also a legal case.
Whole; with nothing missing.
Moving forward; in front.
For the present time; until further notice.
To a great extent; for the most part.
Perhaps; it may be so.
Simultaneously; also.
A company or person that offers a product or service.
To a marked degree; distinctly.
An appointment to a post; also a legal appeal or a vocation.
A company or person that runs a facility or service.
Across the whole country.
During the same time; in the meantime.
A flat area of land; also a level within a structure.
The reaching of an agreement between sides.
Needed or demanded for a purpose.
A rise in amount or level.
The government minister in charge of a country's money.
In this country; here where we are.
Very recently; not long ago.
A statement of grievance; also a legal action in court.
A ticket in a draw; also a person's fate or lot.
Using a particular method or tool.
By the name of; also on behalf of.
The unused ability to develop or succeed in the future.
The repair and improvement of a building or area.
The body that runs the administration of a town or city.
The place where an accident happened.
Happening at once or with nothing in between.
The act of making something available to the public.
Now and then; for limited periods.
To the highest degree; very.
The act of taking control of something, such as a company.
For the most part; mainly.
The act of protecting against attack; also the body's resistance.
A union formed between groups or countries for a shared aim.
A subject or situation that must be dealt with.
Held in high regard by others.
In connection with a particular event.
Something that shows that a condition or event exists.
Stated clearly and directly.
The equipment and features that something is provided with.
An honour or prize given for achievement.
Strong excitement and eager interest.
The effort to fight against or stop something harmful.
To let something stay unchanged.
The state of being ready or willing to act.
In the best possible way; excellently.
Inside a stated period of time.
The way something is shown, described, or represented.
Great respect, or a source of pride and good reputation.
The person who legally owns something.
Kept within limits; reduced.
